✈️ Manage Booking (Qatar)→
Vienna Airport → Langen am Arlberg
Tuesday 16 June 2026 · 6h 36m · Non-stop
🚂 RJX 19970 🪑 Car 23 · Seat 65 🪟 Window 🔇 Quiet Zone €38.40 paid
Your Route — Visual Map
🗺️ Vienna Airport → Langen am Arlberg
✓
14:05 — LAND
✈️ Vienna Airport (VIE) — Terminal 3
Collect bags · proceed to immigration. Have passport + Visa C ready.
Arrive
✓
~14:30–15:00
🛂 Schengen Immigration + Baggage Claim
South African entering Schengen. State purpose: seasonal work. Show: passport + Visa C + work permit + employment contract. Straightforward — be confident, truthful.
Have docs ready
✓
~15:00–15:20
🚶 Walk to Flughafen Wien Bahnhof
The train station is INSIDE the airport. After exiting arrivals hall, follow the red ÖBB signs or "Bahnhof / Train Station" signs. Take escalators/lifts DOWN one level. 5–10 min walk. No extra transport needed.
Station is IN the airport — just follow ÖBB signs ↓
✓
~15:20–16:45
☕ Wait at Station — Eat, SIM, Rest
You have ~1.5hrs. BILLA supermarket + cafes inside. Buy food for the 6.5hr journey. BUY A LOCAL SIM NOW if you haven't — you arrive at 23:38 in a mountain town, you'll need it. Telekom Austria / Drei both have airport shops.
Buy SIM here if you don't have one
✓
16:45 — BE ON PLATFORM
🚉 Find Your Platform — RJX 19970
Check the large departure board for "RJX 19970 Innsbruck/Bregenz". Platform shown on board. Look for a red RailJet train. Board early — find Car 23 (Wagen 23), Seat 65. Luggage rack at end of car or overhead.
Car 23 · Seat 65 · Window 🪟
✓
17:02 🚂 DEPART
Flughafen Wien Bahnhof — Train Departs
You're on board. Quiet Zone rules: phone silent, no loud calls, headphones for music. Conductor will check ticket + ID. Sit back — you're going to the Alps.
Relax — 6h 36m journey
✓
~17:30
Wien Hauptbahnhof (Vienna main station)
Brief stop. STAY ON THE TRAIN. You do not change here. Watch your bags.
✓
~19:00
St. Pölten / Wels
Rolling through the Austrian countryside. Stay on board. Good time to eat.
✓
~20:30
Salzburg area / Attnang-Puchheim
Train turns southwest. Views getting better. About halfway.
✓
~21:30
🇩🇪 Kufstein / Jenbach — Germany Corridor
Train briefly enters German territory (DB Korridor) — this is completely normal. No passport check within Schengen. You'll re-enter Austria shortly. Don't panic.
Normal — no action needed, stay seated
✓
~22:20
🏔️ Innsbruck — Capital of Tyrol
Major stop, surrounded by Alps. Stunning at night. Train continues west toward Vorarlberg. About 1h 20m remaining.
Still 1h 20m to go — stay on board
✓
~23:15
Bludenz — almost there
Next stop is yours. Start gathering your bags now. Make sure you have everything. It's cold outside (5–10°C). Get your warm layer out.
Get ready — next stop is YOURS
✓
23:38 🎯 GET OFF HERE
🏔️ Langen am Arlberg Bahnhof
Small mountain station. Get off with all bags. Bergmähder / Oberlech is ~15km away. Confirm with employer if they're collecting you — if not, Bus 90 toward Lech, or taxi. Address: Oberlech 531, 6764 Lech.
YOUR STOP — GET OFF !
Step-by-Step: Vienna Airport
1
14:05
Land · follow "Gepäckausgabe / Baggage" signs
Terminal 3. Stay calm, follow the crowds.
2
14:10–14:30
Immigration — "Non-EU / Drittstaaten" queue
Show: Passport · Visa C · Work Permit (ABB 4664758) · Employment Contract. Purpose: seasonal employment, Bergmähder Oberlech GmbH.
3
14:30–14:50
Collect checked bags (2 bags)
Baggage carousel — find your flight QR183 on the screen.
4
14:50–15:10
Exit arrivals · Follow ÖBB / Bahnhof signs DOWN
Red ÖBB signs everywhere. Take lift/escalator down. 5–10 min walk. FREE — you don't need a ticket to get to the platform level.
5
15:10–16:45
Station — eat, buy SIM, charge phone
BILLA supermarket, McDonald's, various cafes. Drei / A1 shops for SIM. Charge devices — 6.5hr journey ahead.
6
16:45
Find RJX 19970 on departure board · go to platform
Large screens show platform numbers. RJX = red Railjet train. Find Car 23 (painted on platform/train). Seat 65 is a window seat in the quiet zone.
7
17:02
Train departs — you're on your way to Lech 🎿
Sit back. You've made it through the hardest part. The Alps are waiting.
🌙
Arriving 23:38 — it'll be dark and cold5–10°C in the Alps at night in June. Have a warm layer in your hand luggage, accessible before you get off the train.
📱
Confirm pickup from Langen with employerOberlech 531 is 15km from the station. Either they collect you, or Bus 90 toward Lech, or taxi.

📌 Key Reminders from Your Documents
🧴
Personal consumables are YOUR responsibilityShampoo, soap, dishwasher tablets — buy and organize these yourself. Not provided.
🧺
You wash + dry your own towels & bed sheetsPlenty are provided, but laundering them is each person's own responsibility.
👥
Your teamNatalie (off: Wed + "Tue") — reception, HK lead, laundry. Sabrina — owner/manager, strategy, HR, finance, also does reception. Thomas — maintenance/outdoor. You (Dillon, off: Mon + "Tue") — laundry, uniforms, HK, breakfast/bar, reception (TCA, SALTO cards, sevdesk, social media), facility/grounds, rubbish.
📅
Pre-season ramp-up (until 26 Jun)Normal 5-day week (Sat+Sun off), Mon–Fri 08:00–12:00 & 13:00–17:00. Your first day (17 Jun) is 13:00–17:00. Official summer season + your regular schedule starts 26 Jun.
⏰
3× Tuesday "extra" workdays during the seasonNormally 37h/week with 2 days off — but 3 times this season you'll work an extra Tuesday (44h that week). Keep an eye on the schedule for these.
🏠
Staff apartment2 separate bedrooms + shared bathroom + shared kitchen/common area. Follow the apartment + Bergmähder house rules (posted on the front door). Anything damaged must be replaced — handle shared spaces with care.
🎓
Your first 2 weeks — training schedule17 Jun: intro + coffee machine + admin (Meldezettel, insurance, Evernote app). 18 Jun: breakfast prep + hygiene training + bar training. 19 Jun: laundry/cleaning products/LechWash intro. 23 Jun: BAR training repeat + Brandmeldeanlage (fire alarm system). 27 Jun: wellness prep walkthrough + social media intro.
🚭
Confidentiality + no side jobsDon't discuss business/guest info outside work, even after leaving. No second job (paid or unpaid) without written employer approval — penalty is steep (6× net monthly salary).
👔
Dress code recapReception/Bar/Lounge: provided black trousers + blouse + Birkenstocks. Bring your own: black top under blouse, black socks. Kitchen: add the Bergmähder apron, tie back long hair. HK/Laundry: own comfortable black pants/leggings + Bergmähder shirt, tied-back hair.

Reminder rhythmCheck this page once in the morning (after breakfast shift) and once in the evening before bed. Takes 30 seconds. Builds the habit.

⚡ Your Edge — Tips to Win This Season

You're going somewhere new, in a foreign language, in a small tight-knit team. These will give you the edge.

🕖 Show Up 5 Minutes Early. Always.

In a small hotel team, punctuality is loyalty. Being there before your shift starts tells Sabrina you're dependable. It costs nothing and pays compound interest.

📋 Use This App Every Shift

Before you start — open the Work tab. Run through the timeline for your shift. After you finish — open Spend and log anything you bought. 2 minutes. Makes you systematic and stress-free.

🧠 Learn the Routine Until It's Automatic

The first 2 weeks are about learning the system. Don't try to improvise — follow the task timeline exactly. Once it's in your muscle memory, you'll have mental bandwidth to go above and beyond with guests.

💬 Communicate Proactively

If something breaks, runs out, or looks wrong — tell someone immediately, don't wait. Small team = high trust = no politics. Say what you see. It builds respect fast.

🎿 Use the LechCard — Earn Your Recovery

Your rest days are Monday and Tuesday. Get out. The LechCard gives you free cable cars. Being in the mountains on your days off resets you mentally and keeps you energised for work. Don't spend your days off indoors.

💰 Track Every Euro From Day One

Your salary is €2,026/month gross. After tax you'll take home roughly €1,600–1,700. Housing and LechCard are free. Set a weekly budget and track it here. Save. This season is a financial springboard if you're intentional.

⏰ Master the Morning Shift Timing

The breakfast basket delivery at 08:00 is non-negotiable. Work backwards: baskets prepped by 07:50, coffee machine running by 07:30, bread collected at 07:00. Be a machine. Guests notice when breakfast is perfect.

📱 Social Media = One of Your Jobs

You are responsible for Bergmähder's social media. Document beautiful moments — a perfect breakfast basket, a misty Alpine morning, the wellness area at golden hour. Great content takes 5 minutes and makes the whole property shine.

🧘 Manage the Homesickness Actively

It'll hit. Usually around week 2–3. Call home. Stay connected. But also: invest in the relationships around you. Natalie, Thomas, Sabrina — they're your team. Small hotels build real friendships.

🗂️ Log Working Hours Same Day

Your contract requires you to record hours latest 1 day after working. Do it the same evening. File location: "6.2. Mitarbeiter" in the shared cloud. Late logs = disputes. Accurate logs = trusted employee.

🌡️ The Alpine Cold Is Real

Even in summer, Lech mornings are cold. When you're doing early bread collection or outdoor furniture, you'll feel it. Keep a fleece accessible. It also gets cold fast when the sun goes behind a peak.

🏆 One Simple Goal Per Week

Week 1: Learn the names of every task and where everything is. Week 2: Do every task without being reminded. Week 3: Start anticipating what needs doing before being asked. That's how you become irreplaceable.

🚌 Lech-Zürs Local Bus

Free with your LechCard (provided by your employer). Runs all summer: 18 Jun – 4 Oct 2026.

💳
Your LechCard covers the village shuttle networkLines 701, 702, 704, 709 are free village shuttles. Lines 706 and 707 go to mountain lakes (Spullersee, Formarinsee) and cost extra (toll road) — see fares below.
🗺️ Visual Route Map
701 Rüfiplatz to Stubenbach 702 Dorfhus to Zug 704 Zürs to Lech to Oberlech 709 Lech to Warth to Hochkrumbach 706 to Spullersee (paid) 707 to Formarinsee (paid)
📍
The easy way to think about itRüfiplatz is the central hub where almost every line passes through or starts. If you are ever unsure which bus to catch, get to Rüfiplatz first — from there, every direction in Lech is reachable. Dorfhus is the other major hub, right next to Rüfiplatz, mainly for buses heading to the lakes.
🚏 Bus Lines — Tap to See Full Timetable
704 Zürs ↔ Lech ↔ Oberlech ▾
Runs every 30 minutes, 07:03 to 18:56 (last bus). Free with LechCard
Zürs TrittkopfSeekopfbahnRüfiplatzDorfhusLandbruggBrunnenhofFeuerwehrhausSpitzeneggBurgplateau Jct.Salome Jct.Schlössle Oberlech
This is likely your main commuting line — it connects Lech village directly to Oberlech, where Bergmähder is. On the hour and half-hour from Rüfiplatz.
701 Rüfiplatz ↔ Stubenbach ▾
Runs every 30 minutes, 06:48 to 19:36. Free with LechCard
RüfiplatzDorfhusLandbruggBrunnenhofFeuerwehrhausHaus BernhardMarkus KapelleStubenbach
Heads south through the valley toward Stubenbach — useful for accommodation and residential areas in that direction.
702 Dorfhus ↔ Zug ▾
Runs every 30 minutes, 07:15 to 19:32. Free with LechCard
DorfhusRüfiplatzPlatteSchwimmbad (pool)EggKirchleAlphornZug Mautstelle (toll gate)
Good for reaching the public swimming pool (Schwimmbad) and the village of Zug.
709 Lech ↔ Warth ↔ Hochkrumbach ▾
Runs roughly hourly, 07:37 to 18:00 (and back 09:02 to 18:22). Free with LechCard
SchlosskopfBrunnenhofFeuerwehrhausBodenalpeBürsteggWanghusWarth DorfplatzSteffisalpeJägeralpeWolfeggGh AdlerHochkrumbach
The longest line — goes over to Warth and Hochkrumbach on your days off if you want to explore further afield.
706 Dorfhus ↔ Spullersee (mountain lake) ▾
Hourly-ish, 09:15 to 17:32. €26 Spullersee · €13 Älpele (toll incl.)
DorfhusRüfiplatzZug KirchleZuger Säge (toll)ÄlpeleAbzweigungDalaaser StafelSpullersee
Scenic mountain lake trip for a day off — this one is NOT free, it is a paid toll-road excursion.
707 Dorfhus ↔ Formarinsee (mountain lake) ▾
Roughly hourly, 08:18 to 19:02. €26 Formarinsee · €13 Älpele (toll incl.)
SchlosskopfDorfhusRüfiplatzZug KirchleZuger Säge (toll)ÄlpeleAbzweigungJohannesbachwegFormarinsee
Another scenic lake day-trip option, also paid. Beautiful but worth budgeting for.
🐕
Dog rule on all busesLeash and muzzle required for dogs — not relevant to you, but good to know if a colleague brings one.
Contact & Resources
🚌 Ortsbus Lech+43 5583 2213-226 ℹ️ Lech Zürs Tourismus+43 5583 2161 🌐 lechzuers.com→

♻️ Rubbish & Recycling Schedule

Lech municipality collection days — Summer 2026, effective from 22 June.

⚠️
Sort carefully — especially bio wasteThe municipality specifically asks that bio waste is kept strictly separate from general waste, otherwise it causes problems with their composting process.
🗑️
Reminder: you're the rubbish-responsible team memberPer your work info, bins get collected next to the house. Put bins out on collection days — if you're off-shift that day, arrange for someone else on the team to do it.
📅 Weekly Collection Days
🗑️
General Waste (Restabfall)
Every Monday
Non-recyclable household waste — general bin
🍌
Bio Waste (Bioabfall)
Every Friday
Food scraps, peels, organic matter — keep strictly separate from general waste
📰
Paper (Altpapier)
Every Tuesday
Newspaper, cardboard, paper packaging
🍾
Glass (Glas Verpackungen)
Every Wednesday
Bottles & glass packaging — see specific colour-sorted dates below
🥤
Plastic (Kunststoff Verpackungen)
Every Wednesday
Plastic packaging — bags, bottles, tubs
🍾 Glass — Specific Collection Dates
🌈 Coloured Glass (Buntglas)
01 Jul · 22 Jul · 12 Aug · 23 Sep · 14 Oct
⚪ White/Clear Glass (Weißglas)
01 Jul · 05 Aug · 09 Sep (continues — check community noticeboard for full list)
🥤 Plastic — Thursday Collection Dates
25 Jun · 09 Jul · 06 Aug · 20 Aug · 03 Sep · 17 Sep
📌
Bottom line for your day-to-dayMon = general waste, Tue = paper, Wed = glass + plastic, Fri = bio waste. The specific colour-sorted glass and plastic dates above are extra collection days — your weekly Wednesday glass/plastic bin still goes out as normal every week.
Official Source
🏛️
Issued by the Gemeinde (municipality) of LechSigned by the Bürgermeister (mayor), Gerhard Lucian. This schedule applies until the autumn plan is announced.

🗣️ German Basics

Vorarlberg dialect varies, but Standard German gets you everywhere. Tap any card to reveal the meaning.

💡
Vorarlberg tipLocals often greet with "Servus" or "Hoi" (informal hello/bye) and "Grüß Gott" (formal hello). "Danke schön" and "Bitte schön" go a long way with guests and colleagues.
